Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or advanced renal disease. Blood glucose and HbA1c are not elevated, making diabetes unlikely. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying renal pathology.
